commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=dedc49c82efa570415091dd8d9de5225bdaf4489)
commands.review_summary:
The PR widens WhatsApp Web append-upsert auto-reply grace after reconnect, adds focused regression tests, and updates WhatsApp docs plus CHANGELOG.

PR surface: Source +38, Tests +54, Docs +1. Total +93 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main still skips append messages older than the fixed 60 second pre-connect guard, and the linked issue describes reconnect message loss. No live-device reproduction is present in this PR.
commands.review_followup: This needs contributor real-device proof, a rebase, and a maintainer decision on default reconnect catch-up replies versus the related opt-in config path; automation cannot supply the contributor's WhatsApp environment proof.

target.title: [codex] fix WhatsApp reconnect catch-up replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep the existing short stale-history guard for initial WhatsApp Web startup history sync.
- After reconnecting from a recently active WhatsApp listener, allow recent `messages.upsert(type="append")` catch-up messages to enter the auto-reply path within the existing message watchdog window.
- Add regression coverage for the inbox append grace window and the reconnect handoff from the Web monitor.
- Document the reconnect catch-up behavior in the WhatsApp runtime model and add a changelog entry.

## Root Cause

OpenClaw already received Baileys `append` upserts for history/offline catch-up, but `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts` only auto-replied when the message timestamp was within 60 seconds before the new socket's connect time. During a stale socket/reconnect window, a real inbound message can be several minutes older than the replacement socket's connect time. In that case OpenClaw marked the message read and skipped auto-reply as stale startup history.

Refs #50093.

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.test-harness.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.append-upsert.test-support.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Notes

I did not do a live WhatsApp-device replay because the original miss was observed on a personal local setup and GitHub auth required pushing through a fork. The focused tests cover the exact skipped `append` window and the reconnect option wiring.
target.author: vishalj99

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: fix(cli): preserve Discord voice outbound helper
target.body:
Summary
- Gate CLI lazy outbound dependency factories on known channel IDs instead of hardcoding Discord voice helper names in core CLI routing.
- Preserve explicitly supplied `discordVoice` / `sendDiscordVoice` helpers while leaving missing helpers unresolved so the Discord runtime can decide the voice path.
- Add focused regression coverage for the CLI deps proxy and outbound send mapping for #84952.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 4 files, 109 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `git diff --check FETCH_HEAD...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`

Signed head: `c122749f86a53c1b720167a443b976b59991f403`.

Behavior addressed: Discord cron announces with `messages.tts.auto="always"` could fail before the Discord voice-message helper ran because the CLI outbound deps proxy manufactured a fake channel adapter for the `discordVoice` helper lookup.

Review feedback addressed: ClawSweeper's maintainer option suggested replacing the helper-specific deny list with a generic known-channel-id guard. This head now uses the channel registry normalizer before resolving lazy outbound factories, so helper-like non-channel keys stay unresolved without embedding Discord-specific helper literals in core CLI routing.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu source checkout on Node `v22.22.3` with focused CLI, Discord outbound adapter, and cron double-announce regression tests. Crabbox/live Discord delivery was not rerun for this refresh.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: The CLI deps regression asserts `createDefaultDeps().discordVoice` and `createDefaultDeps().sendDiscordVoice` remain `undefined` instead of becoming unavailable channel adapters, and the outbound mapping regression asserts lazy channel factories are not called for those helper keys while explicit helpers are still preserved. The implementation achieves that through known channel ID resolution rather than a Discord-specific deny list.

Observed result after fix: Focused regression suite passed: 4 files, 109 tests passed. Formatting, lint, diff checks, and the branch has an SSH-signed head commit.

What was not tested: A fresh live Discord cron announce with generated TTS audio was not run in this refresh; the existing live Discord proof remains in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85529#issuecomment-4529709599.

Fixes #84952

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: fix(cli): preserve Discord voice outbound helper
target.body:
Summary
- Gate CLI lazy outbound dependency factories on known channel IDs instead of hardcoding Discord voice helper names in core CLI routing.
- Preserve explicitly supplied `discordVoice` / `sendDiscordVoice` helpers while leaving missing helpers unresolved so the Discord runtime can decide the voice path.
- Add focused regression coverage for the CLI deps proxy and outbound send mapping for #84952.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 4 files, 109 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `git diff --check FETCH_HEAD...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`

Signed head: `c122749f86a53c1b720167a443b976b59991f403`.

Behavior addressed: Discord cron announces with `messages.tts.auto="always"` could fail before the Discord voice-message helper ran because the CLI outbound deps proxy manufactured a fake channel adapter for the `discordVoice` helper lookup.

Review feedback addressed: ClawSweeper's maintainer option suggested replacing the helper-specific deny list with a generic known-channel-id guard. This head now uses the channel registry normalizer before resolving lazy outbound factories, so helper-like non-channel keys stay unresolved without embedding Discord-specific helper literals in core CLI routing.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu source checkout on Node `v22.22.3` with focused CLI, Discord outbound adapter, and cron double-announce regression tests. Crabbox/live Discord delivery was not rerun for this refresh.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: The CLI deps regression asserts `createDefaultDeps().discordVoice` and `createDefaultDeps().sendDiscordVoice` remain `undefined` instead of becoming unavailable channel adapters, and the outbound mapping regression asserts lazy channel factories are not called for those helper keys while explicit helpers are still preserved. The implementation achieves that through known channel ID resolution rather than a Discord-specific deny list.

Observed result after fix: Focused regression suite passed: 4 files, 109 tests passed. Formatting, lint, diff checks, and the branch has an SSH-signed head commit.

What was not tested: A fresh live Discord cron announce with generated TTS audio was not run in this refresh; the existing live Discord proof remains in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85529#issuecomment-4529709599.

Fixes #84952
